_comment: The ideal closed-loop homestead OUTLINE (one person). Operator directive 2026-07-05: 'use Home as a page for outlining what we need in the perfect ideal 100% closed loop self-sustaining homestead... it could also help us clearly outline what we need in the game for the home.' Every number is distilled from docs/design/homestead-solo-design.md (sections 0-9), whose figures were cross-checked against the live game data (home.ron catalog, component_outputs.ron, plants.csv) and AGREE. Every game_id below is a real machine/structure id in the game data. Rendered by BOTH the native Home page (src/gui/pages/homes.rs, 'The ideal closed loop' panel) and the web mirror (web/pages/home.html fetches /data/home_outline.json). Infinite-of-X: edit here, both surfaces update, no code change. Lives at data/ TOP LEVEL because the VPS deploy only syncs top-level data JSON to the public web root (subdirs like data/coordination/ stay private). No em dashes in copy (operator rule).
title: The ideal closed-loop homestead
subtitle: One person. Five survival loops that close, five gaps that honestly cannot. This outline is both the real-world recipe and the game's requirements list for Home.
intro: 100% closure is physically impossible (entropy always leaks), but a single person CAN close the survival loops: energy, water, air, nutrients, and nearly all food. Everything below is sized for one adult from real figures, and every component already exists in the game's data. What one home cannot close is listed last, on purpose: those gaps are why community and trade exist.

loops.name: Power
loops.demand: 4.0 kWh/day, duty-cycle honest (life support 0.6, water handling 0.17, cooking 1.0, cold storage 0.8, lights and tools the rest)
loops.closure_note: 4 panels yield about 5.76 kWh/day real (400 W x 4.5 sun-hours x 0.80 system losses), a 44% headroom over demand. Winter sun can halve; the battery bank plus a backup generator carry the gap. The biodigester can brew that generator's methane from your own waste, closing a slice of the fuel loop too.

requirements.why: 400 W each; 1.44 kWh/day real yield per panel, not nameplate
8 kWh usable, about 2 days of autonomy
0.7 kWh/day; keeps trickling when the sun does not
covers the winter deficit; burns biodigester methane

loops.name: Water
loops.demand: 80 L/day net household (drink, cook, wash); irrigation is NOT net demand because greywater recycling covers it
loops.closure_note: An 8,000 L cistern holds 100 days of autonomy for one person, and passive rain catchment adds about 144 L/day for free. Dry-season storage is the binding constraint, so the oversized tank is a feature, not waste.

requirements.why: 8,000 L storage = 100 dry days; rain catchment included
2 L/min lift refills a day's use in about 40 minutes
240 L/day potable polish, 3x the one-person need
the tap/shower/sink node; cistern to pump to purifier to taps

loops.name: Food
loops.demand: 2,200 kcal/day complete nutrition (about 55 g protein), one adult
loops.closure_note: The honest cap is light, not shelf space: free sunlight is a single canopy, so a room's output is bounded by its lit floor area no matter how many towers you stack. About 700 to 1,000 m2 of intensive growing feeds one person; the 34x34 m garden room is 1,156 m2, so the same garden that fell short for three people genuinely closes for one. Indoor alone reaches about 2,078 kcal/day (94%); one outdoor grain field plus one legume field push it into surplus, and the silo banks 750 days of buffer.

requirements.why: 960 kcal/day of bulk calories, aeroponic, NASA-real
828 kcal/day; all vegetables, vitamins, and plant protein across 32 real crops
75 kcal/day of fats (sunflower, flax)
100 kcal/day from dark vertical space; needs no light budget
closes B12 and omega-3, the two nutrients plants cannot give you
token indoor cereal; worst per-m2 crop, kept minimal on purpose
3,100 kcal/day outdoor surplus, dried and stored
700 kcal/day protein AND fixes nitrogen for the grain field
750 days stored; the winter and bad-harvest buffer

loops.name: Air
loops.demand: 0.55 kg O2/day plus matching CO2 scrubbing (sealed-habitat sizing; on open Earth this loop is free ventilation)
loops.closure_note: The garden is the primary life support: 1,156 m2 of plant canopy makes far more oxygen than one person needs. The 25 W recycler is the backup, and it is priority 1 on the power bus for a reason: cut power long enough and air is the first loop to fail.

requirements.why: 25 W, sized for three people, so one person has deep margin
the food loop IS the oxygen loop; bioregenerative and free

loops.name: Nutrients and waste
loops.demand: about 1.5 kg/day of organic waste (food scraps, crop residue, humanure) returned to the soil
loops.closure_note: This is the loop-closer: recycling waste lowers every other loop's demand. Compost plus urine diversion return 587% of the nitrogen, 533% of the phosphorus, and 197% of the potassium the crops need, so even the tightest nutrient is in surplus. Fish waste feeds the towers; the legume field fixes nitrogen; the biodigester turns the rest into generator fuel.

requirements.why: 1.2 kg/day nutrient plus 155 L/day greywater back to irrigation; the second bin bridges the 60-90 day maturation lag
methane for the backup generator plus digestate fertilizer
blackwater handling for the wetroom

loops.name: Shelter
loops.demand: one insulated, walkable home wrapping all of the above: garden room, wetroom, kitchen, workshop, storage, sleep
loops.closure_note: The house is the container that makes every loop's numbers work: insulation shrinks the heat budget, the sealed envelope makes air a real loop, and room layout is the plumbing and power topology. Every wall, fixture, and furniture piece in the design is a real buildable id in the game today.

requirements.why: the room layout blueprint; the solo build uses fewer rooms of the same shells
sink, shower, toilet from the standard structures catalog
cook and preserve the harvest; cold storage is the 0.8 kWh/day line in the power budget
repairs keep every other loop's machines alive

cannot_close.note: Five loops stay open no matter how good the homestead is: electronics and semiconductors, metal from raw ore, medicine synthesis, equipment replacement, and raw chemistry inputs. They are listed in full in the panel below this outline. The gap is not a failure; it is why neighbors, trade, and civilization exist.

in_game_next: Play-load the solo home: walk into this exact build as a playable save (the data for it already exists as the home_solo variant).
Progress persistence: the Home page's live balances should track YOUR home as you build it, loop by loop, against this outline.
Real-home import: measure your actual house against the ideal (the long-term homes-as-profiles direction).
footer: Everything in this outline exists in the game's data today. The outline IS the requirements list: when the in-game Home can be built, powered, watered, fed, breathed, and composted to match this page, the Home feature is done enough to teach the real thing.
